name: Headless Pi vs Vis proof task suite on zai-coding/GLM-5.1

description: Start gates passed: Pi smoke, Vis JSON model smoke, verify quick, selected P1/Task 5A.

asi.hypothesis: Before comparing P1, prove benchmark substrate is fair: same ZAI GLM model available in Pi and Vis, quick repo gate clean, selected P1 guard/gate harness task.
asi.autoresearch_doc_status: autoresearch.md missing; user explicitly designated PROOF_AUTORESEARCH.md as boss document and forbade extra committed markdown artifacts.
asi.selected_task: P1 / PROOF.md Task 5A pure guard/gate evidence harness.
asi.trust_boundary: extension aggregate/cache/status/checkpoint remain mutable state; only runtime-derived immutable observations may feed evidence.

description: Probe Pi JSON output shape for token extraction before P1 comparison.

asi.hypothesis: Pi --mode json output may expose usage/tokens directly; need runtime truth before building P1 scorer.
asi.finding: Probe output did not parse with current fallback and printed numeric-looking chunks; token extraction for Pi should use session/event artifacts or robust fallback, not assume .usage.total_tokens.
asi.next_action_hint: Run P1 comparison with full output captured and parse tokens opportunistically; do not block proof comparison solely on missing Pi token field.

description: P1 comparison harness crashed because isolated PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR lacked ZAI model settings.

asi.hypothesis: Disposable Pi session directory would keep all runtime artifacts under target while preserving model access.
asi.rollback_reason: Harness set PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR to target, hiding configured zai-coding model/provider; Pi reported model not found before benchmark ran.
asi.next_action_hint: Use default PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR with --no-session for Pi benchmark, or copy non-secret settings safely if isolation is needed. Start gate proves provider itself works.

description: P1 Pi comparison timed out at 300s while still using tools; no completed assistant answer to score.

asi.hypothesis: Retrying P1 with default Pi config would avoid the provider-isolation crash and produce a fair baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: Pi headless GLM-5.1 run did not reach final answer within 300s; only partial tool-use transcript existed.
asi.next_action_hint: Use longer timeout, reduce prompt scope, or implement PROOF.md directly before resuming optimization.

description: P1 Pi comparison retry timed out at 600s; benchmark blocked by agent runtime latency.

asi.hypothesis: Increasing the timeout to 600s would let the same P1 Pi comparison complete after provider isolation was fixed.
asi.rollback_reason: Pi still timed out before final assistant answer; comparison cannot be scored reliably.
asi.next_action_hint: Pause optimization loop and implement PROOF.md slices directly, then resume autoresearch on smaller completed-task prompts.

description: Implement PROOF.md direct proof spine through ledger and evidence bundle derivation before resuming Pi/Vis comparison.

asi.hypothesis: Because headless Pi comparison timed out, the best next Vis improvement is to implement the missing proof spine directly: canonical proof namespace migration, pure guard/gate harness, immutable provenance_event ledger, and evidence_bundle derivation from runtime events only.
asi.benchmark_scope: Implementation-gate proxy for original vis_loss while comparison is blocked; this is not a Vis-beats-Pi claim and not a replacement for later suite comparison.

lessons_learned: Attestation path leaves conversation_intent_gate_ref at 0 even when a candidate proof was offered; evidence authority lives in evidence_bundle_member.
State-gate proof stores evidence-bundle-id as a string while evidence bundle API returns UUID ids.
asi.next_action_hint: Task 26: migrate remaining db-prove-gate! ref-rejection tests to evidence bundle creation/attestation path or move canonical-ref validation to accepted evidence requirements, then hard-error/delete legacy writer APIs.

description: Task 26 persistence ref-rejection migration failed on compact-ref error-code expectation.

asi.hypothesis: Migrate remaining persistence core db-prove-gate! ref-rejection tests to evidence-bundle/attestation behavior so persistence tests no longer depend on legacy prove/fulfill writers.
asi.rollback_reason: Persistence core test failed because compact evidence/from-ref i1.1 is rejected by spec shape first with :invalid-requirement, not by derive-binding's :non-canonical-ref branch. Saved patch to target/proof-autoresearch/safety/task26-before-invalid-requirement-fix.patch before revert.
asi.failure_details: Lazytest failure at core_test.clj:1220 expected :non-canonical-ref, actual :invalid-requirement.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply saved patch, update compact-ref bundle expectation to :invalid-requirement or relax to membership in #{:invalid-requirement :non-canonical-ref}, then rerun persistence/foundation tests and verify quick.

description: Add Task 26 migration of persistence ref-rejection tests off legacy prove writer.

asi.hypothesis: Migrate remaining persistence core db-prove-gate! ref-rejection tests to evidence-bundle/attestation behavior so persistence tests no longer depend on legacy prove/fulfill writers.
asi.benchmark_scope: Implementation-gate proxy while Pi comparison remains blocked by headless GLM timeout; not a Vis-beats-Pi claim.
verification: fresh REPL reload of vis.core/sqlite core
task26 grep smoke: persistence core test has zero db-prove-gate! and zero db-fulfill-intent! references; contains compact evidence-bundle rejection, db-attest-gate!, and non-successful-event checks
asi.trust_boundary: Persistence tests no longer exercise legacy proof/fulfill write authority. Ref invalidity is now represented as rejected evidence bundles or rejected gate attestations, while blocker-compatible timeout evidence still uses the blocker path.
asi.compatibility_note: The legacy db-prove-gate!/db-fulfill-intent! functions and public exports still exist in source/delegates for external compatibility; internal tests are migrated off them.
lessons_learned: Compact evidence/from-ref fails evidence requirement spec first, producing :invalid-requirement rather than :non-canonical-ref.
Unobserved canonical refs in db-create-evidence-bundle! currently produce a rejected bundle with :non-successful-event because missing DB rows are represented as placeholder events by ref.
asi.next_action_hint: Task 27: hard-error or delete legacy db-prove-gate!/db-fulfill-intent! write authority and remove public core/delegate exports if final grep shows no internal caller still requires them.

name: Vis cold startup latency with lazy voice extension loading

description: Baseline cold bin/vis help startup with all classpath extensions including voice.

asi.hypothesis: Cold Vis startup is slow because extension discovery loads every extension namespace, including voice namespaces with sherpa/ONNX classes on the classpath. Establish CLI help baseline before changing load path.
asi.user_symptom: TUI/Vis takes too long to load; suspected extension and voice model loading.

description: Temp no-voice classpath probe failed because generated deps.edn was malformed.

asi.hypothesis: Removing vis-voice from the root classpath should distinguish Clojure/JVM startup from voice dependency or namespace load cost.
asi.rollback_reason: The temp deps generator removed lines containing vis-voice, leaving malformed EDN in aliases/deps shape; process failed before startup benchmark was meaningful.
asi.next_action_hint: Use REPL manifest timing and targeted namespace require timing instead of malformed temp deps; or generate a proper minimal deps.edn from deps.edn data.

description: Temp no-voice classpath probe did not improve cold help startup.

asi.hypothesis: If voice dependency/classpath presence dominates cold startup, removing com.blockether/vis-voice from deps should make bin/vis help substantially faster.
asi.rollback_reason: No improvement: temp classpath without voice measured 8137.6ms vs 7871.1ms baseline. Voice dependency presence is not the main cold Clojure startup cost.
asi.next_action_hint: Optimize actual extension discovery and TUI path; voice model/inference should still be lazily loaded for correctness/headless safety, but it is not the 8s cold CLI bottleneck.

description: Defer voice ASR/input namespace loading until voice status/download or TUI voice hook invocation.

asi.hypothesis: Voice extension core currently requires ASR/input namespaces during manifest discovery; deferring those with requiring-resolve should reduce extension discovery work and prevent sherpa/ONNX/Java Sound voice paths from loading until first voice use.
asi.result: Startup improved from 7871.1ms baseline to 7702.6ms. Small but real directionally; measurement still dominated by cold Clojure/JVM and full manifest discovery, not voice models.
asi.user_request: Defer voice model loading until first use.

description: Probe TUI channel help startup after lazy voice core change; not an improvement over current primary baseline.

asi.hypothesis: TUI channel command help may better represent TUI load path than top-level help and expose channel-specific startup cost.
asi.rollback_reason: This was a probe using a different, heavier command (bin/vis channels tui --help), so 9936.1ms is not comparable to top-level help and is worse than the active primary metric.
asi.next_action_hint: Use top-level cold startup as primary for this experiment segment, and treat TUI-help timing as side information; if switching to TUI-specific target, reinitialize experiment with TUI command as baseline.
asi.finding: TUI channel help main total was ~4651ms vs top-level help ~3166ms, indicating channel path adds startup work beyond manifest discovery.

description: Static TUI voice command attempt failed formatting/parse gate.

asi.hypothesis: TUI enumerates extension commands during startup; returning a static Ctrl+B command and lazily resolving voice input only when run should avoid loading voice input/ASR during TUI command enumeration.
asi.rollback_reason: ./verify.sh --quick failed because the edited voice core test could not be parsed/formatted; likely delimiter imbalance in the test edit.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the static hook change with a corrected core_test.clj form, run clj-paren-repair if needed, then rerun voice tests and verify quick.

description: Static TUI voice command avoided parse issue but did not improve top-level cold startup metric.

asi.hypothesis: TUI enumerates extension commands during startup; returning a static Ctrl+B command and lazy-resolving voice input only when the command runs should avoid loading voice input/ASR during TUI command enumeration.
asi.rollback_reason: Top-level bin/vis help startup metric got worse (11824.3ms vs best 7702.6ms/current baseline 7871.1ms). This metric does not exercise TUI command enumeration, so it cannot justify keeping under the current primary benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: If optimizing actual TUI startup next, reinitialize autoresearch with a TUI-specific benchmark that invokes channel command enumeration or launches TUI help; then reapply static hook under that target.
asi.finding: The static hook is functionally correct but not visible in the top-level help benchmark.

description: Skip extension discovery for root-only help requests.

asi.hypothesis: Root-level help does not need extension namespaces; skipping discover-all for no-arg/help/--help/-h should cut Vis cold startup without changing real command execution or TUI paths.
asi.result: Startup improved from 7702.6ms best/7871.1ms baseline to 6381.5ms for root help. This removes full extension discovery from root help only; non-root commands still discover extensions before dispatch.
asi.scope: Optimizes Vis CLI root load. TUI startup still pays extension discovery and needs separate optimization.

description: TUI startup baseline run was interrupted before a metric could be captured.

asi.hypothesis: Switch primary target from root help to TUI channel help because user specifically reports TUI/Vis load latency.
asi.rollback_reason: run_experiment returned aborted before output/metric capture, so no valid baseline exists for this new TUI startup segment.
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun TUI startup baseline with the same command and log the parsed tui_startup_ms before applying another code change.

description: Establish valid TUI channel help cold startup baseline after interrupted run.

asi.hypothesis: Use `bin/vis channels tui --help` as a headless-safe proxy for the TUI command path: it must discover/register the TUI channel without launching Lanterna full-screen.
asi.result: Valid baseline captured at 7670.5ms after previous aborted run. JVM main total ~3390ms; shell cold Clojure process ~7628ms.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect manifest require timings and reduce work needed before TUI command dispatch, without removing extension functionality from real TUI launch.
